topStories.title: US-Israel-Iran conflict: 'This is not Europe's war'—EU nations rebuff Trump's call to send warships to Hormuz Strait
topStories.description: Europe has made it clear that it is not going to engage in the US and Israel's war in Iran, adding that Americans chose this path. Luxembourg's Deputy Minister went a step further with his rebuke of Donald Trump, stating that they will not be ‘blackmailed’ by Washington.

topStories.title: US Stocks: Delta Air Lines lifts revenue outlook despite jet fuel hit, shares jump 4%
topStories.description: Delta Air Lines raised its first-quarter revenue forecast in light of robust travel demand, offsetting a hit from spiking jet fuel costs due to the Middle East war.

dailyNews.title: Google’s Personal Intelligence feature is expanding to all US users
dailyNews.description: Personal Intelligence allows Google's AI assistant to tap into your Google ecosystem, such as Gmail and Google Photos, to provide more tailored responses.

dailyNews.title: Stryker says it’s restoring systems after pro-Iran hackers wiped thousands of employee devices
dailyNews.description: The hack, which brought ongoing widespread disruption to the company's operations, is thought to be the first major cyberattack in the United States in response to the Trump administration's war in Iran.

dailyNews.title: Chile's president begins building border barrier less than week into term
dailyNews.description: José Antonio Kast visited the ditch - a few feet wide and deep - on Monday, calling it a "milestone".

dailyNews.title: St. Patrick’s Day 2026 in US: What’s open, what’s closed, and key details
dailyNews.description: St. Patrick’s Day dates back to the 17th century when it was established as a religious feast day.

dailyNews.description: OpenAI has reportedly signed a partnership with AWS to sell its AI systems to the U.S. government for classified and unclassified work, marking an expansion beyond its Pentagon deal last month.

dailyNews.title: Actress responds to Daryl Hannah's scathing criticism of her portrayal in JFK Jr. series, 'Love Story'

dailyNews.title: Employee crushed to death in horror industrial accident after press machine unexpectedly turns on
dailyNews.description: Ohio Ford employee Gregory Knopf, 61, reportedly died after being crushed by a malfunctioning press machine at Sharonville transmission plant Monday.

dailyNews.title: Alabama 'preparing to play' NCAA Tournament without star guard Aden Holloway after felony drug arrest
dailyNews.description: Alabama coach Nate Oats said the team will begin the NCAA Tournament without suspended star Aden Holloway following his drug arrest Monday morning. He is the second-leading scorer.

dailyNews.description: The Denver Broncos reportedly acquired star receiver Jaylen Waddle from the Miami Dolphins in a blockbuster trade, adding a major offensive weapon to their roster.

dailyNews.title: What’s in Trump’s SAVE Act, the Voter ID Bill Republicans Are Pushing?
dailyNews.description: The legislation would require voters to prove their citizenship in person upon registration, ban IDs without a photo at polling places and criminalize failures to enforce such requirements.

dailyNews.title: M.T.A. Sues Trump Administration to Release 2nd Avenue Subway Funding
dailyNews.description: New York transit officials are seeking nearly $60 million in overdue federal funding to extend the subway line to East Harlem. The administration’s rationale for the freeze has been inconsistent.

dailyNews.title: Trump Officials Weigh New Plan to Stop Offshore Wind Farms
dailyNews.description: Proposed settlements would block wind farms off New York State and North Carolina, according to documents reviewed by The New York Times.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Gutting of Election Security Fuels Worries for Midterms
dailyNews.description: Officials say the crippling of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, which the president himself created, could open elections to cyberattacks and foreign influence.

dailyNews.title: Trump Signals He May Intervene in Cuba Following Iran and Venezuela
dailyNews.description: Still at war with Iran and in control in Venezuela, President Trump is signaling that he is about to intervene in another country.

dailyNews.title: U.S. counterterrorism director Joe Kent resigns over war: 'Iran posed no imminent threat'
dailyNews.description: "Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation," Kent wrote in his letter to President Donald Trump.

dailyNews.title: Senate Democrats send DHS counteroffer to Trump as shutdown drags on
dailyNews.description: The Department of Homeland Security shutdown has caused pileups in airport security lines across the country.

dailyNews.title: Mastercard says it's acquiring stablecoin startup BVNK in $1.8 billion bet on future of payments
dailyNews.description: Stablecoin startups have been a hot commodity since the reelection of President Donald Trump in late 2024 ushered in a new era of crypto-friendly regulation.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he thinks he will have the 'honor' of 'taking Cuba'
dailyNews.description: The president recently launched a war in Iran after topping Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro by force.

dailyNews.title: Trump signals coalition to force open Strait of Hormuz is not ready yet: 'Some are less than enthusiastic'
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump said Monday that some U.S. allies are not willing to join a coalition to protect tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Trump: 'We don't need any help' with Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: President Trump said the United States does not need help with securing the Strait of Hormuz amid the conflict with Iran but also said NATO allies "don't want to help us."

dailyNews.title: National Counterterrorism Center director resigns over Iran war
dailyNews.description: The director of the National Counterterrorism Center, a retired Green Beret and longtime supporter of President Donald Trump, says he has resigned over the war in Iran.
